short: An AI hardware company whose LPU chip runs AI models very fast, with little delay.
definition: Groq is an AI hardware company, founded in 2016 by former Google engineers, that designs the Language Processing Unit (LPU), a custom chip built only to run trained AI models (not train them). Its predictable, step-by-step design avoids the unpredictable timing of general-purpose graphics chips (GPUs), so it generates words quickly and with little delay. Groq runs GroqCloud, a service that lets developers use open models on LPU hardware.
